title: Raciborskanthos krabiensis
intro: Raciborskanthos krabiensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Raciborskanthos krabiensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to aid in digestion and relieve menstrual cramps.
